state_profiles.agricultural_highlights: California mixes permanent crops in the Central Valley with coastal vegetables—one county may peak months away from a neighbor.
fun_facts: California grows almost all U.S. almonds and most leafy greens in winter.
Napa and Sonoma wines anchor a global brand.

resource_links.label: California Department of Food and Agriculture

state_profiles.agricultural_highlights: Island supply chains favor diversified truck crops on windward slopes and seed production on Molokaʻi.
fun_facts: Kona coffee remains one of the world’s priciest specialty origins when farmed at elevation.
Taro loʻi still anchor Native Hawaiian food sovereignty efforts.
